articles.title: Ecma International Approves ECMAScript 2025: What’s New?
articles.description: It’s that time of year again. The Ecma General Assembly has approved the ES2025 language specification, which you can read in full here if you have a gallon of coffee to hand — or you can enjoy Dr. Axel’s more succinct explainer instead.
articles.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer

articles.title: What's Coming to JavaScript
articles.description: The ES2025 spec is great, but what else is coming down the pike? The Deno team has put together a look, complete with code samples, at nine proposals progressing through TC39’s process right now.
articles.author: Casonato and Jiang (Deno)

articles.title: Vite 7.0 Released
articles.description: At five years old, Vite has radically changed the frontend building experience and is an essential tool for many. v7 is an evolutionary step more than a revolutionary one and should prove an easy upgrade from v6.
articles.author: VoidZero Inc.

articles.title: Tips for Making Regular Expressions Easier to Use in JavaScript
articles.description: Dr. Axel asks us to imagine if we had to write JavaScript without any whitespace or comments, so why should we have to write regexes that way? He has some tips for making the process more pleasant.
articles.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer

articles.title: Creating a Simple RSS Server Side Reader
articles.description: Alex likes that blogs have feeds, but isn’t so keen on modern feed readers, so he put together a surprisingly simple Deno-based approach to scrape feeds and produce an automatically updated HTML page linking to the latest items.
articles.author: Alex Kladov

articles.title: Hono 4.8: A Cross-Runtime Standards-Oriented Web Framework
articles.description: Hono is a framework well worth exploring. It’s fast, lightweight, built on Web Standards, and can be used to build apps that work on numerous platforms from Node or Bun to Cloudflare or Fastly. v4.8 adds new route helper functions, improvements to JSX streaming and CORS, a new plugin system for static site generation, and more.
articles.author: Yusuke Wada and Contributors

articles.title: LogTape 1.0.0: Universal Logging for JavaScript Apps
articles.description: Whether it’s in Node, the browser, or edge functions, LogTape is here for you. It’s particularly aimed at library developers who want to add logging in a low hassle way for end users – learn more here.
articles.author: Hong Minhee

articles.title: 🤖 Google Unveils Gemini CLI: An Open-Source AI Agent
articles.description: Google has dipped its toe into the rapidly growing AI dev agent game with a terminal-based agent, built in TypeScript, with a striking high free usage allowance, making it a good way to give such tools a try if you haven’t so far.
articles.author: Mullen and Salva (Google)

articles.title: PLJS 1.0: JavaScript Language Plugin for Postgres
articles.description: PLV8 has been the ‘go to’ way to use JavaScript as a procedural language within Postgres for years, but this QuickJS-based variant, from the same maintainer, has a far lighter footprint, is easier to maintain, and may be enough for your needs.
articles.author: Jerry Sievert

articles.title: Marked 16.0: A Fast Markdown Parser and Compiler
articles.description: Demo here to see it in action. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Christopher Jeffrey

articles.title: Vue Infinity: Virtualized Rendering for Large Datasets
articles.description: The idea is simple: if it’s not visible, it doesn’t get rendered. A handy way to keep things fast when dealing with large feeds, carousels, dashboards, etc. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Isaac Tewolde

articles.title: Spark: Advanced 3D Gaussian Splatting Renderer for Three.js
articles.description: Check out the live examples.
articles.author: World Labs Technologies, Inc.

articles.description: The speaker lineup for JSConf 2025 has been announced. It takes place in Maryland this October.
If you love the V8 team's deep dives, they have a new one looking at two new optimizations introduced into V8 for speeding up WebAssembly.

Shopify's William Candillon reflects on the future of advanced graphics in React Native using React Native Skia, and how WebGPU, Three.js, and a new backend called Skia Graphite are changing the game.
The popular and long standing Construct HTML5 + JavaScript game development environment now has full built-in support for TypeScript too.
The Angular team has created a set of LLM prompts and AI IDE rules files to help Angular developers get better code out of LLMs.
Vercel hosted its annual Vercel Ship event this week and they made so many announcements, you'll need this recap post to get through them all.
🕒 The Temporal API proposal is now a Stage 3 Draft.
Transformers.js 3.6 – Run machine learning models in the browser, Node, Deno & Bun. Now supporting Gemma 3n (though not in-browser yet).
zx 8.6 – Google's tool for better Node shell scripting.
Node.js v22.17.0 (LTS), v24.3.0 (Current) and v20.19.3 (LTS)
Prettier 3.6, Bun v1.2.17, SVGO 4.0

articles.title: A Guide to Scroll-Driven Animations with Just CSS
articles.description: Here’s a look at how to start using scroll-driven animations via CSS (using scroll() and view()), with no JavaScript required. There’s code examples here highlighting how this technique can used to create progress bars or animate elements into the viewport. Saron also notes accessibility and motion considerations.
articles.author: Saron Yitbarek

articles.title: A Short History of Web Bots and Bot Detection Techniques
articles.description: A solid high-level overview of the cat-and-mouse game of web bots and various detection techniques. Looks at the evolution of bots so far, along with IP reputation, fingerprinting, headless browser flaws, proxy detection, CAPTCHAs, behavioral analysis, and more. Good stuff.
articles.author: OlegWock

articles.title: Astro 5.10 Released
articles.description: The main point of interest in this release is that Astro’s responsive image feature is now production ready. Additionally this version adds a new experimental fetch feature for real-time, dynamic content, along with improvements to CSP support.
articles.author: Matt Kane

articles.title: Firefox 140.0: New Features, Updates and Fixes
articles.description: This new version of Firefox arrived just yesterday, adding a new vertical tabs feature along with support for the Custom Highlight API, the SVG fetchpriority attribute, and more. Developer release notes here.
articles.author: Mozilla

articles.title: Liquid Glass, But in CSS
articles.description: Here’s Apple’s new Liquid Glass design language, but realised with CSS. Compatibility here isn’t great, but on the whole it’s a fun, yet hard-earned bit of experimentation.
articles.author: Pup Atlas

articles.title: Lightly Poking at the CSS if() Function in Chrome 137
articles.description: Support for this powerful function shipped recently in Chrome and Edge — here Sunkanmi takes a high-level look at the syntax, how it works, and how we may want to use it with other properties.
articles.author: Sunkanmi Fafowora

articles.title: Color Everything in CSS
articles.description: Juan aims to give us “a big enough picture for some technicalities behind color in CSS, such that you feel confident using functions like lab() or oklch() while also understanding what makes them special”.
articles.author: Juan Diego Rodríguez

articles.title: CSS Cascade Layers vs. BEM vs. Utility Classes: Specificity Control
articles.description: Specificity can often be the cause of some unpredictable CSS behaviour, as such Victor breaks things down for us so we don’t have to lean on !important flags.
articles.author: Victor Ayomipo

articles.title: How JPEG Became the Internet’s Image Standard
articles.description: Thirty years ago the JPEG pretty much became the dominant way to share digital images online — it wasn’t the first image format for the web, and it’s by no means the last. Ernie takes us on a history lesson.
articles.author: Ernie Smith

articles.title: A Better API for the Intersection and Mutation Observers
articles.description: Zell refactors both the Mutation and Intersection Observers into something “way simpler to use”.
articles.author: Zell Liew

articles.title: Mossaik: A Web-Based Tool to Create Stylish SVG Backgrounds
articles.description: Choose from a number of preset shapes including waves, polygons, circles, blobs, etc. There’s also numerous settings for each shape to edit to your needs.
articles.author: Gabriel Perales

articles.title: <syntax-highlight>: A Custom Element for Syntax Highlighting
articles.description: A custom element that uses the CSS Custom Highlight API (supported by most modern browsers) for syntax highlighting so you don’t need to retreat the time-old method of wrapping every token in spans. GitHub repo.
articles.author: André Ruffert

articles.title: Browser MCP: Connect AI Apps to Your Browser to Automate Tests and Tasks
articles.description: Includes an MCP server plus Chrome extension that enables AI apps like VS Code, Claude, Cursor, and Windsurf to do tasks like repeatedly fill out a form or test user flows. Repo.
articles.author: Browser MCP

articles.title: Contrast Report: A Simple WCAG Colour Contrast Checker
articles.description: Select either WCAG AA or AAA guidelines and the tool lets you generate a shareable link. One of the neat features is the “picture-in-picture” mode that uses an eyedropper for color selection.
articles.author: Adam Chaboryk
